verdict_note: Code defects 4.2/4.3/4.8/4.4/4.9 are reproduced-then-fixed with red-green tests; Postgres was live-verified on a disposable postgres:16 (fixing a real autocommit durability bug). The pinned Lean v4.28.0 + Mathlib v4.28.0 kernel is BUILT, the Aristotle path is live-verified against the real service (fixing TWO real async/archive SDK-adapter bugs), and the SEALED local Lean kernel replay works end-to-end (scenario 5 PASS). Production wiring: distinct per-branch worker roles (4.11), durable content-addressed model-exchange artifacts + signed events (4.10), model-proposed finite experiments executed in the hardened sandbox reaching at most COMPUTATIONAL_EVIDENCE (4.5), the full typed worker-output schema (4.1: proof steps, assumptions, formalization requests, Lean declaration candidates), and the formal-candidate path wired into `egmra run` (4.6: RunnerWorker emits formal_candidates with deterministic hashes; `egmra run --lean-project` builds a real LeanService whose kernel runner is the pinned checker, which re-checks the declaration with the REAL Lean kernel). `egmra run --formal-correspondence-review <signed.json>` now consumes an independently signed formal-correspondence review so a kernel-verified Lean declaration is admitted as a claim-bound KERNEL_CHECKED formal proof of the informal claim (signing stays out-of-band in the independent review domain; the CLI only consumes and re-verifies). Audit follow-up (fresh live audit): fixed the largest end-to-end blocker — branch leases are now HEARTBEATED while a (slow) browser worker reasons, so long-but-live generation is no longer discarded as `stale_worker_rejected: lease has expired`; fixed the live OEIS parser to accept the current top-level JSON-array response shape; the referee's `formal_audit` now checks a real kernel artifact instead of `informal_only`, and `egmra run` sets `informal_only=False` when a Lean project is configured; and parsed `proof_steps`/`assumptions`/`formalization_requests` are now carried on WorkerOutput and recorded in the durable branch memory (no longer dropped). Full suite: 1016 passed, 3 skipped, rc=0. Verdict stays INCOMPLETE: genuine asynchronous multi-tab browser workers are implemented (one Chromium + N tabs on a dedicated event loop; `egmra campaign --provider browser --workers N`) and hermetically proven, but the live async Playwright page driver is verified live (not in CI); autonomous Aristotle formalization is now wired into `egmra run --formalizer aristotle` (the vendor produces the proof for a pinned declaration+type obligation, re-checked by the pinned kernel; the live SDK path is verified live, and campaign wiring is analogous follow-up); still open are production scholarly/theorem-library retrieval; and no LIVE browser->Lean->FORMALLY_VERIFIED_CANDIDATE run has been demonstrated (the referee still requires a genuine diverse verification: an executable countermodel + independent replay, which a single mock/one-model run cannot fake).

test_baseline.note: Observed 1016 passed, 3 skipped, rc=0 without a Postgres server; the 3 skipped live-Postgres tests are gated on EGMRA_TEST_POSTGRES_DSN and pass with one (1019 total). Live Aristotle (submit/fetch + autonomous formalize), live Lean-kernel sealing, the live formal bridge, and the live async multi-tab browser page driver are driven manually with a real key/toolchain/profile; hermetic tests cover the contracts.

scenarios.name: False statement -> CANDIDATE_DISPROOF
scenarios.note: Controlled false fixture with an executable predicate. Arbitrary free-text has no synthesized predicate, so free-text false statements land OPEN/BLOCKED, not a counterexample.

scenarios.name: Ambiguous + injected formal/external evidence stays BLOCKED_BY_INTERPRETATION
scenarios.command: egmra run --erdos 312 --provider deterministic --policy signed-policy.json ; pytest egmra/tests/test_result_states.py

scenarios.name: Finite computation -> COMPUTATIONAL_EVIDENCE
scenarios.observed: classifier maps a finite exact-computation-supported goal to COMPUTATIONAL_EVIDENCE, distinct from a general proof
scenarios.note: The full fixture CLI path to COMPUTATIONAL_EVIDENCE additionally requires the signed --intent-review artifact; without it the honest state is PARTIAL_PROGRESS.

scenarios.name: Known theorem through browser + Lean -> FORMALLY_VERIFIED_CANDIDATE
scenarios.reason: The pinned Mathlib (v4.28.0) kernel is BUILT, the SEALED local-replay checker is OPERATIONAL (scenario 5), the formal-candidate path is wired into `egmra run --lean-project` (4.6), and `egmra run --formal-correspondence-review <signed.json>` now consumes an independently signed formal-correspondence review so a kernel-verified Lean declaration becomes a claim-bound KERNEL_CHECKED formal proof of the informal claim (verified end-to-end through research(): the goal reaches truth_status=SUPPORTED with formal_verification=KERNEL_CHECKED and the correspondence certificate bound). Browser reasoning is separately live-verified. Remaining to reach the PUBLIC FORMALLY_VERIFIED_CANDIDATE state: (a) a live authenticated browser emitting valid Lean, and (b) the independent adversarial referee observing a genuinely passing verification (all 10 required attacks complete + no defects + model-family/replay independence) — a real trust boundary that a single mock checker deliberately cannot fake, and which the correspondence flag does not control.
scenarios.activation: run `egmra run --lean-project <built> --intent-review <signed> --formal-correspondence-review <signed>` with a browser worker emitting a valid Lean candidate + expected type; then satisfy the independent adversarial referee via a genuine diverse verification (real replay independence across environments), not a mock.

scenarios.name: Aristotle submit/poll/download + local Lean replay + sealed attestation
scenarios.command: egmra formalize --formalizer aristotle --declaration egmra_live_check --expected-type '2 + 2 = 4' --prompt '...' ; (or the AristotleSdkClient + LeanReplayVerifier drivers)
scenarios.observed: Full chain LIVE-verified with the operator key: submit -> poll (queued->in_progress->complete) -> download -> safe-extract (promotable=false) -> SEALED local Lean kernel replay attestation. The pinned checker (egmra/lean/kernel_checker.py) runs the REAL kernel via `lake env lean` on a definitional obligation `example : 2 + 2 = 4 := @egmra_live_check` plus `#print axioms`, computes candidate_type_hash from the exact intended type (no blind echo), AttestedKernelRunner signs a verdict that verify_for accepts, and LeanReplayVerifier sealed a LocalLeanReplayAttestation (claim goal, Lean 4.28.0, Mathlib v4.28.0) bound to the source-tree hash. A vendor COMPLETE is never trusted; only the sealed local replay is promotable. Two SDK-adapter bugs (fully-async SDK; get_files writes an archive to a file) were found-and-fixed by the live round-trip.
scenarios.note: Sealed-checker interface work is DONE: source_root + expected_type_source added to CheckerRequest; canonical type-hash binding (egmra/lean/kernel_checker.py) with 11 hermetic tests + the live seal. Reachable from the CLI via `egmra formalize --formalizer aristotle|local`.

scenarios.name: Five workers, real overlap, no dup/skip, fencing, bounded
scenarios.command: egmra campaign --erdos-range 5-14 --provider deterministic --workers 5 --policy signed-policy.json --state s.json ; pytest egmra/tests/test_campaign.py
scenarios.observed: concurrency report max_observed_concurrency=5, distinct_workers=5 (w0..w4), no duplicate/skipped problems; Barrier(5) test proves genuine simultaneity.
scenarios.note: Deterministic provider supports real 5-worker concurrency. Browser provider is capped to --workers 1: Playwright's sync API is single-threaded; multi-tab async browser concurrency is documented future work.

scenarios.name: Rate limiting: shared cooldown, <=120s, retain on outage, resume
scenarios.observed: SharedThrottle cross-worker cooldown persists + clamps to 120s + honors Retry-After; BrowserProviderUnavailable retains the job (exit 4), never a math failure; wired into the browser provider via _browser_throttle().
scenarios.note: Simulated throttling verified. Live browser throttling not exercised (needs sustained live load).

scenarios.name: Kill and restart resume without skip/dup; stale output rejected
scenarios.observed: durable HMAC-signed campaign state resumes with no skip/dup; stale fencing tokens rejected; tampered state fails closed

scenarios.name: Live retrieval + OEIS frozen packet with provenance
scenarios.command: egmra run --erdos 1 --provider deterministic --policy signed-policy.json --retrieval corpus --oeis offline
scenarios.observed: The CLI now builds an auditable retrieval corpus (613 TheoremRecords from the packaged Erdos snapshot; each has source_uri=erdosproblems.com/N, sha256 content hash, and a verbatim statement extract) and constructs an OEISClient, both passed into research() -> the RetrievalService freezes a provenance-bearing solver packet handed to the worker after the blind cold pass. Run output reports retrieval.corpus_records=613. An OEIS/retrieval match seeds conjectures only and never promotes proof status (enforced by ImportAuditor + separate novelty query log).
scenarios.note: Offline retrieval+packet-with-provenance verified live. Live oeis.org fetch is reachable via --oeis live but a network fetch was not exercised this session. Tests: egmra/tests/test_erdos_corpus.py, test_cli.py::test_cli_run_arbitrary_wires_retrieval_corpus_and_oeis.

scenarios.name: Clean wheel runs all CLI paths outside the checkout
scenarios.command: pip wheel . --no-deps -w /tmp/w ; pip install <wheel> in a fresh venv ; cd /tmp ; egmra --help/doctor/run --erdos 312 --provider deterministic
scenarios.observed: wheel self-contained (corpus packaged, no erdos_common/erdos_ingest); egmra run --erdos 312 outside checkout -> BLOCKED_BY_INTERPRETATION, rc=0

scenarios.name: PostgreSQL concurrent append/reconnect/replay/resume/rollback
scenarios.command: docker run -d --name egmra-pg -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=*** -e POSTGRES_DB=egmra -p 5433:5432 postgres:16 ; export EGMRA_POSTGRES_DSN=... ; egmra init-db ; egmra run --erdos 1 --event-store postgres --provider deterministic --policy signed-policy.json ; egmra verify-events --event-store postgres --run-id erdos-1 ; EGMRA_TEST_POSTGRES_DSN=... pytest egmra/tests/test_m2_postgres_conformance.py
scenarios.observed: Live on a disposable postgres:16 (port 5433, the developer's own :5432 untouched): init-db created the events table (UNIQUE(run_id,sequence)); egmra run --event-store postgres persisted the research event chain to Postgres (2 rows, seq 0..1); a FRESH process (egmra verify-events) reconnected and replayed with integrity=true and reproduced the merkle head; the live pytest suite proves append/len/events, reconnect+replay-merkle parity, transaction rollback leaves no partial write, and out-of-band payload tamper -> verify_integrity=false. init-db/verify-events log only a credential-redacted DSN.
scenarios.note: A live durability bug was found-and-fixed by this acceptance: reusing a non-autocommit connection left the initial COUNT's implicit transaction open, demoting each append's transaction() to a savepoint that was rolled back on close(); fixed by switching the reused connection to autocommit so every append is a durable top-level BEGIN/COMMIT. Multi-worker campaign-on-postgres is supported (each attempt opens its own PostgresEventStore/connection); a 5-worker concurrent campaign against Postgres was not separately run this session.

scenarios.name: Security adversarial rejections
scenarios.observed: rejects: malicious archives (traversal/symlink/bomb/oversize), pre-existing symlink quarantine dest (inside+outside root, lstat no-follow), forged/stale checker attestations, wrong-claim + wrong-target Lean proofs, stale fencing tokens, tampered campaign checkpoints

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Fake five-worker concurrency
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Campaign.drain hardcoded worker_ids[0]. Added Campaign.run_concurrent (threads, per-worker leases, monotonic fencing, heartbeats, in-process+fcntl lock) + _concurrency_report. CLI campaign uses it. Barrier(5) test proves real overlap.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Shared rate limiting not wired
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: _browser_throttle() builds a durable SharedThrottle for the browser provider; _build_runner passes it to BrowserChatGPTRunner in both run and campaign.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Aristotle quarantine symlink-follow
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: resolve_quarantine_dir() now lstat-checks the root and the job-dir child WITHOUT following, joins job_id onto the resolved root without resolving the child, and verify_local_replay rescans the tree for symlinks immediately before replay.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Live retrieval + OEIS wired into the production CLI
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Added build_erdos_corpus() (parses the packaged Erdos TeX into auditable TheoremRecords with source_uri, sha256 content hash, and verbatim statement extract; dedupes by problem number). The CLI run/campaign build the retrieval corpus (--retrieval corpus|none) and an OEISClient (--oeis auto|offline|live) and pass both into research(), so the RetrievalService freezes a provenance-bearing solver packet and the OEIS sequence-search stage is reachable. An OEIS/retrieval match never promotes proof status. Live-verified: egmra run reported retrieval.corpus_records=613.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: --event-store jsonl|postgres wired into CLI + init-db/migrate-db
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: PostgresEventStore is now a full EventLog drop-in (reused autocommit connection, events property, __len__, last_event_id, close, migrate) consumed by research() via a new event_log= injection. CLI adds --event-store jsonl|postgres and --dsn (DSN from --dsn or EGMRA_POSTGRES_DSN, never logged; only a credential-redacted DSN is printed), plus egmra init-db and egmra migrate-db (idempotent schema), and egmra verify-events --event-store postgres. Live-verified end-to-end on a disposable postgres:16: append/reconnect/replay/rollback/tamper. Found-and-fixed a real durability bug (savepoint demotion on a reused non-autocommit connection).

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Aristotle SDK adapter called the async official SDK synchronously
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: The official aristotlelib SDK is fully async (every Project/AgentTask method is a coroutine), but AristotleSdkClient called them synchronously — it only worked against the synchronous test fake. Found by the live round-trip. Fixed: the adapter now runs each awaitable to completion on a persistent client-owned event loop (non-awaitable values from the fake pass through unchanged), with a close() teardown.
tests: egmra/tests/test_aristotle_sdk.py::test_client_drives_async_sdk_submit_poll_fetch (async fake mirroring the real coroutine shape)

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Aristotle fetch assumed get_files populates a directory
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: The real SDK's get_files(destination) writes a SINGLE tar.gz archive blob to a FILE path (default *_aristotle.tar.gz), but the adapter (and both fakes) passed a directory and scanned it — raising IsADirectoryError against the real service. Found by the live round-trip. Fixed: download the archive to a temp file, then safe_extract_archive the bytes into the quarantine dir under strict traversal/symlink/bomb limits, then re-scan. Both fakes updated to emit a real tar.gz so tests exercise the true contract.
tests: egmra/tests/test_aristotle_sdk.py (fetch quarantine + symlink-in-archive rejection + async fake)

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Sealed local Lean kernel checker + `egmra formalize` (scenario 5)
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Added source_root + expected_type_source to CheckerRequest (threaded via LeanReplayTarget/LeanReplayVerifier) and a real pinned checker (egmra/lean/kernel_checker.py): it binds the attestation to the source-tree hash, rejects sorry/native escapes, runs the REAL Lean kernel via `lake env lean` on a definitional obligation (example : <expected_type> := @<decl>) + `#print axioms`, and computes candidate_type_hash from the exact intended type so verify_for's candidate_type_hash==expected_type_hash is sound (no blind echo). write_pinned_checker pins the checker with the EGMRA-capable interpreter. `egmra formalize --formalizer local|aristotle` makes it reachable. Live-verified: sealed a LocalLeanReplayAttestation over the actual downloaded Aristotle proof AND via `egmra formalize --formalizer local`.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Distinct worker roles per mechanism branch, wired to allocation
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: research() now assigns a distinct method-specific role per mechanism branch (prover / experimentalist / formalizer via WORKER_ROLE_BY_FAMILY), records it in each branch's mechanism_fingerprint and memory, and drives the worker with it via RunnerWorker.for_role() so the prompt is role-specific. Branches already carried distinct fingerprints, posteriors, and read-slices; the role is the added allocation dimension.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Durable content-addressed model-exchange artifacts + signed events
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Added a MODEL_EXCHANGE_RECORDED event action and _record_model_exchanges(): research() (given an artifact_store, which the CLI now constructs) persists each model/browser exchange's provenance to the content-addressed ContentAddressedObjectStore and appends a signed event referencing the artifact hash. Raw response text is never stored (only hashes/URL/provenance). Idempotent across shared runners.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Model experiment proposals executed via the trusted sandbox
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: RunnerWorker now accepts an optional capability-free `experiment(inputs)` in a model's experiment proposal and executes it via a shared _execute_finite_experiment() in the ComputeService's hardened RestrictedPython/container sandbox; research() generalizes trusted_compute_service to any worker owning a compute service and re-authenticates the artifact + independent replay. The CLI gives the RunnerWorker a ComputeService. A finite check yields at most COMPUTATIONAL_EVIDENCE (admitted exact_computation evidence) and never proves a general claim — the sound boundary is enforced and tested.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Full typed worker-output schema
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: parse_worker_response now captures proof_steps, assumptions, formalization_requests, and lean_declaration_candidates ({claim_id, declaration_name, source, expected_type}) in addition to claims/falsifiers/queries/sequences/experiments; incomplete Lean candidates are dropped (never a fabricated formal artifact). branch_prompt requests the full schema.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Formal-candidate path wired into egmra run (worker emits formal_candidates; real LeanService)
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: RunnerWorker converts lean_declaration_candidates into WorkerOutput.formal_candidates, computing every hash deterministically (expected_type_hash is the canonical hash the pinned checker recomputes, so candidate_type_hash==expected_type_hash is sound; project_hash + immutable_target_module_hash are derived, never model-trusted). `egmra run --lean-project` builds a real LeanService whose kernel runner is the pinned checker; LeanService.verify_declaration writes the candidate into a hardened quarantine and binds source_root so the REAL Lean kernel (lake env lean) re-checks the declaration. Verified live via the LeanService bridge (kernel_verified + target_type_matches on a real proof). A verified declaration is still NOT admitted as a formal proof of the informal claim without an independently signed formal-correspondence review (enforced).

defects_fixed_this_session.title: --formal-correspondence-review CLI flag consumes an independently signed correspondence review
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Added `egmra run --formal-correspondence-review [CLAIM_ID=]PATH` (repeatable; CLAIM_ID defaults to 'goal') and _load_formal_correspondence_reviews(), mirroring --intent-review: it loads signed FormalCorrespondenceCertificate JSON artifacts (regular non-symlink file, <=1MB, no duplicate JSON keys, no duplicate claim) and hands them to research() as the claim-keyed formal_correspondence_reviews mapping. Signing stays out-of-band in the independent review domain (sign_formal_correspondence_certificate, already present); the CLI only CONSUMES and the orchestrator re-verifies the signature. Verified end-to-end through research(): a CLI-loaded signed review discharges the formal_correspondence_required blocker and admits the kernel-verified declaration as a claim-bound KERNEL_CHECKED formal proof (goal SUPPORTED, formal_verification=KERNEL_CHECKED, correspondence certificate bound). This does NOT bypass the independent adversarial referee, which remains the gate to the public FORMALLY_VERIFIED_CANDIDATE state.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Branch lease heartbeat around (slow) browser generation
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: The largest end-to-end blocker from the fresh live audit: a browser run made 12 genuine ChatGPT exchanges across 3 branches, then discarded every branch as `stale_worker_rejected:<branch>:lease has expired`. Branch leases had a hard 60s grace with NO heartbeat, and the fence is only checked AFTER the worker returns; modern browser reasoning commonly exceeds 60s, so valid work was rejected as stale. Fix: loop._lease_heartbeat() runs a daemon thread that renews the branch lease every 20s (< the 60s grace) while branch_worker.work_branch() runs, so long-but-live reasoning is preserved. Safety is unchanged: if the branch is legitimately superseded (a replacement worker bumps the fencing token), the renewal fails closed and the post-return fence check still rejects the stale batch. Verified hermetically with an injected clock (heartbeated lease survives past grace; un-heartbeated lease expires; superseded lease is still rejected).

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Live OEIS top-level JSON-array response parser
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Live OEIS now returns a bare top-level JSON array; the client required an object containing `results`, so a fresh Catalan-number query failed with OEISUnavailable. Fix: OEISClient._parse_response normalizes a top-level array to {"results": [...]} (object form preserved), so both live and cached paths see one consistent shape. Non-object array entries are still rejected.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Referee formal_audit checks the kernel artifact; CLI sets informal_only for formal runs
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: MechanicalAttackEvaluator's `formal_audit` attack tested `informal_only` (so a genuine formal run, informal_only=False, ALWAYS failed the audit and blocked release). Fix: formal_audit now passes when the run is informal-only (no formal obligation) OR the graph holds a valid Lean/ATP proof evidence bound to a formal-correspondence certificate (a real local kernel/formal audit artifact). Also: `egmra run` now sets informal_only=False when a Lean project (--lean-project) is configured, so a formal run is audited as one. Verified via the full formal chain: formal_audit fails when no kernel proof is admitted (pass 1) and passes once the correspondence-bound kernel evidence is admitted (pass 2).

defects_fixed_this_session.title: proof_steps/assumptions/formalization_requests carried on WorkerOutput
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: parse_worker_response captured proof_steps, assumptions, and formalization_requests but RunnerWorker.work_branch dropped them when building WorkerOutput. Fix: WorkerOutput gained those three fields, RunnerWorker populates them, and research() records them in the durable per-branch problem_local memory alongside failures/falsifiers so the informal reasoning trail is consumed and auditable (deeper claim-graph semantics — assumptions as claim hypotheses, formalization_requests driving the formal path — remain future work).

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Genuine asynchronous multi-tab browser workers
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Playwright's sync API is single-threaded, so the browser provider previously ran one worker and campaigns rejected browser --workers>1. New egmra/agents/async_browser.py drives ONE authenticated Chromium via the ASYNC Playwright API on a dedicated event-loop thread with a POOL of N pages (tabs): AsyncBrowserEngine marshals each worker thread's synchronous BrowserBackend call onto the shared loop with run_coroutine_threadsafe, so N tabs make genuine concurrent progress on one account (the SharedThrottle still coordinates cooldowns). TabBackend binds one page per worker; build_browser_runner_pool builds one BrowserChatGPTRunner per tab. `egmra campaign --provider browser --workers N` now builds the engine + pool and assigns each worker its own tab (the --workers 1 cap is removed); the engine is torn down after the campaign. Per-page DOM ops live behind the async AsyncPageDriver seam; the live PlaywrightAsyncPageDriver is a faithful async port of the sync chatgpt_browser helpers, verified live (not in CI), the same posture as the sync backend. `egmra run` remains single-problem-sequential (a single problem's branches are researched in order), so multi-tab concurrency is a campaign feature (run --workers help clarified). Hermetically proven: an asyncio.Barrier fake driver shows all N tabs run simultaneously on the shared loop, and a 2-worker browser campaign assigns two distinct tab-runners with both workers forced active at once.

defects_fixed_this_session.title: Autonomous Aristotle formalization inside egmra run
defects_fixed_this_session.detail: Aristotle was only reachable as a standalone `egmra formalize`. New egmra/lean/formalizer.py adds a Formalizer seam + AristotleFormalizer that turns a PINNED obligation (declaration name + intended Lean type) into candidate Lean source via the Aristotle SDK (submit -> fetch -> read the quarantined .lean). RunnerWorker gained an optional `formalizer`: a model-emitted lean_declaration_candidate may now be source-LESS (a pinned formalization request; parse_worker_response makes proof source optional but still requires declaration_name + expected_type), and when a formalizer is configured the vendor produces the PROOF while the obligation stays pinned + deterministically hashed here. `egmra run --formalizer aristotle` (requires --lean-project + ARISTOTLE_API_KEY) builds the AristotleFormalizer and hands it to the worker; the produced Lean is UNTRUSTED and re-checked by the pinned kernel on the existing 4.6 path (a vendor COMPLETE never promotes; a formal-correspondence review is still required to prove the informal claim). The live SDK path is verified live (not in CI), like `egmra formalize`. Campaign wiring is analogous follow-up. A formalizer outage is recorded (formalizer_error/formalization_unavailable), never a mathematical failure.

remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.title: Autonomous Aristotle formalization inside campaigns
remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.detail: `egmra run --formalizer aristotle` is wired (autonomous), but the campaign path does not yet build a per-worker AristotleFormalizer; wiring it into cmd_campaign (one formalizer per worker, closed with the browser engine) is the analogous follow-up.

remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.title: Production scholarly / theorem-library retrieval
remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.detail: Retrieval is limited to the packaged Erdos snapshot. There is no arXiv, Semantic Scholar, Crossref, MathOverflow, citation-graph, or live theorem-library retrieval pipeline.

remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.title: Live browser->Lean->FORMALLY_VERIFIED_CANDIDATE
remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.detail: The lease-heartbeat fix should let a live browser run retain branch work, and the formal-correspondence + formal_audit wiring is in place, but no live browser->Lean run has reached FORMALLY_VERIFIED_CANDIDATE. The referee still requires a genuine diverse verification (executable countermodel + independent replay), which a single mock/one-model run cannot fake.

remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.title: Rate limits appearing DURING generation are not detected
remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.detail: Pre-submission cooldown is adaptive/capped at 120s, but a rate limit that appears mid-generation is not detected, and after six cooldowns the run exits provider-unavailable (retained, never a math verdict) rather than durably continuing the individual run.

remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.title: DB-backed campaign leases/checkpoints
remaining_defects_not_addressed_this_ses.detail: Campaign state remains a signed local JSON file even when event logs use PostgreSQL; campaign leases/checkpoints are not database-backed.
